worked_example_ref.problem_statement_digest: numeric_range.__getitem__ returns an empty range when sliced with a negative step (e.g. [::-1]), even though reversed() works on the same range. With a negative step, the default None start/stop must be adjusted (start -> last index, stop -> before-first) so the slice walks backwards. Fix numeric_range slicing so a negative-step slice returns the correctly reversed sub-range.

lesson_body: When implementing slice semantics on a custom sequence, None bounds have direction-dependent defaults: a negative step reverses which end is the implicit start and stop, so manual None-checks will always miss an edge. Delegate to the language's built-in slice normalization to get correct clamped, direction-aware indices before mapping them into the sequence's coordinate space.
